subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Mercury: The Swift Planet
description: A quick win: understand Mercury: The Swift Planet.

hook.text: Mercury orbits the Sun in 88 days but rotates so slowly that one day lasts 59 Earth days.

buildup.text: It's the smallest planet—barely larger than our Moon. Its battered surface is covered in craters.

discovery.text: Temperatures swing from 430°C by day to −180°C at night. No atmosphere means no heat retention.

twist.text: Despite being closest to the Sun, Mercury is not the hottest planet. Venus holds that record.

climax.text: NASA's MESSENGER found ice in permanently shadowed craters near Mercury's poles.

punchline.text: Closest to the Sun, yet hiding ice in its shadows.

quiz.question: Why isn't Mercury the hottest planet despite being closest to the Sun?
options: It has no atmosphere to trap heat, while Venus has a thick heat-trapping atmosphere
Mercury reflects most sunlight back into space
It spins too fast for heat to build up
